name: Callback Phishing (Vishing) Detection
description: Detects and responds to callback phishing (vishing) attacks where emails instruct victims to call a fraudulent phone number. AI analysis identifies callback phishing patterns such as fake invoices, subscription renewals, or tech support scams. Suspicious phone numbers are flagged, extracted IOCs are logged, and the SOC is alerted. The email is quarantined and an advisory is sent to employees. Addresses the increasingly common callback phishing attack vector that bypasses traditional URL filtering.
author: T1 Agentics

data.title: Callback Phishing Alert
data.summary: Triggers on callback phishing or vishing-pattern email alerts

data.title: Extract Phone Numbers
data.summary: Parse phone numbers and callback indicators from the email body

script.code:
import re

def main(alert):
    body = alert.get('body', '') + ' ' + alert.get('html_body', '')
    phone_patterns = [
        r'\+?1?[-.]?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}',
        r'\+?\d{1,3}[-.]?\d{3,4}[-.]?\d{3,4}[-.]?\d{0,4}'
    ]
    phones = []
    for pattern in phone_patterns:
        phones.extend(re.findall(pattern, body))
    phones = list(set(phones))
    callback_keywords = ['call', 'contact', 'reach', 'dial', 'phone', 'toll-free', 'helpline', 'support']
    urgency_keywords = ['urgent', 'immediately', 'expire', 'cancel', 'charge', 'invoice', 'renewal', 'subscription']
    found_callback = [kw for kw in callback_keywords if kw.lower() in body.lower()]
    found_urgency = [kw for kw in urgency_keywords if kw.lower() in body.lower()]
    return {
        'phone_numbers': phones,
        'phone_count': len(phones),
        'callback_keywords': found_callback,
        'urgency_keywords': found_urgency,
        'has_callback_pattern': len(phones) > 0 and len(found_callback) > 0
    }

data.title: AI Callback Phishing Analysis
data.summary: Evaluate email for callback phishing tactics and social engineering

config.custom_instructions: Focus on callback/vishing phishing patterns: fake invoice or subscription notices requesting the victim call a phone number, tech support scam patterns, lack of URLs (replaced by phone numbers), urgency to call before a deadline, and impersonation of known brands

data.summary: Is this a confirmed callback phishing attempt?
